title: Cyclosorus productus
intro: Cyclosorus productus, also known as the Asian water fern, is a type of vascular plant that belongs to the family Thelypteridaceae. Traditionally, the plant has been used in medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, diarrhea, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Cyclosorus productus include flavonoids, saponins, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health conditions, including digestive issues and skin infections.
taxonomy: Cyclosorus productus is classified within the family Thelypteridaceae, which is a group of ferns commonly known as the marsh ferns or wood ferns. It is native to Southeast Asia and belongs to the genus Cyclosorus. The genus Cyclosorus is a part of the larger family of ferns and is characterized by its distinctive leaf morphology and growth habits. Cyclosorus productus specifically falls within the subfamily Thelypterioideae, which includes various species of ferns found in tropical and subtropical regions. The classification of Cyclosorus productus is continually being refined as new research emerges.

parts: Cyclosorus productus, commonly known as the autumn fern or autumn lady fern, is a species of flowering plant in the family Blechnaceae. The medicinal parts of this plant are its leaves, which have been used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions. The leaves are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties, making them potentially useful in treating conditions such as arthritis and other inflammatory diseases.
